summary: All 7 round-1 findings correctly addressed. 45 tests (was 40). New tests for T8-01, T8-02, T8-06 are structurally sound and exercise real behavior. T8-04 fix adds 402 body error-field assertions to the parametrized mapping test — the right change. T8-03 (AJV) correctly deferred to task 9. T8-05 and T8-07 fixed cleanly. Two bonus tests added (recovered-signer binding, InvalidPriceError) — both test real behavior and add genuine regression protection. No new issues found.

round1_dispositions.verification: New test at line 343: 'factory-state RPC error with non-stale cache returns last-good value (passthrough)'. Warms the cache on first request (asserts passthrough), rejects all subsequent readContract within TTL, makes second request, asserts passthrough. Directly exercises the `servedStale=true` fallback path in readFactoryState. Litmus test passes: removing the stale-fallback block from core.ts would make this test fail.

New test at line 608: 'replay-store entry is retained after settlement failure — retry with same nonce returns nonce_already_used'. The test drives the real NonceStore via __getNonceStoreForTests(). The verifySpy reimplements the minimal store-insertion contract (checkAndInsert) rather than bypassing it, which means the test exercises the actual synchronous-block TOCTOU primitive. Three independent assertions: (1) first request 402 with receipt_reverted, (2) store.has() == true after settle failure, (3) second request 402 nonce_already_used + settleSpy NOT called a second time. The store.has() call uses the correct HasInput interface ({from, nonce, now}). This is a strong test — deleting the 'leave entry intact' behavior from the store would break assertion (2) and (3).
tasks/9.md owns the AJV schema fixture and the schema-validated 402 body test. Deferral is correct. No action required in task 8.

Parametrized verify-error mapping test at line 479 now asserts `expect(result).toMatchObject({ kind: '402', body: { error: verifyReason } })` alongside the emit-count check. The test title was updated to '... AND 402 body.error matches'. This closes the regression gap where core could mis-read the VerifyResult field and the switch would fall through silently — the 402 body assertion would fail loudly.

Test at line 186 title changed to 'non-base64 bytes in X-PAYMENT returns 400 malformed_payment_header + emits malformed_header phase:json'. Comment documents why this pins to 'json' (Node's Buffer.from silently drops non-base64 chars). Phase assertion is now exact: `{ phase: 'json' }`. The permissive regex is gone.

New test at line 301: 'non-zero vault address is NOT re-fetched after TTL expiry'. Uses fake timers, advances 6000ms, counts readContract calls with functionName === 'vaults'. Asserts count stays at 1 despite TTL expiry for the paused() flag. Directly tests the D3-immutable vault-address caching invariant.

Test at line 77 ('sets X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE header on passthrough; route handler runs and is the sole sender') replaces the vacuous sentByPreHandler flag with a routeHandlerCalls spy. Asserts routeHandlerCalls called exactly once. Real assertion now proves the preHandler did not short-circuit.

new_tests_assessment.name: core passes verifyResult.recoveredFrom to settle (NOT payload.authorization.from)
new_tests_assessment.assessment: GOOD — tests genuine security-critical behavior: the recovered (cryptographically authenticated) address must be passed to settle, not the wire-claimed `authorization.from`. Uses two distinct addresses (wireClaimed != recovered) and asserts both the settle call arg and the X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE.payer field. Litmus test passes: if core passed the wire-claimed address instead, assertions on `settleSpy.mock.calls[0][1]` and `decoded.payer` would both fail.

new_tests_assessment.name: invalid opts.price throws InvalidPriceError (NOT TypeError)
new_tests_assessment.assessment: GOOD — tests the specific error class so a regression that changed the exception type from InvalidPriceError to a generic TypeError would be caught. The error type is observable behavior (callers can catch-by-type). Minor note: the test passes price '-1' which is the simplest invalid value, but this is sufficient — the full price parsing edge case table is in x402.test.ts per the tech-spec testing strategy.

remaining_open_items.description: AJV validation of 402 body against vendored x402 v1 JSON Schema. Confirmed deferred to task 9 scope.

decision_matrix.rationale: All round-1 findings resolved. No new issues found. Remaining AJV schema validation is correctly deferred to task 9 per spec. Test count: 45 core + 4 node-http + 4 fastify + 4 index = 57 tests. All tests exercise real behavior — no mock-return anti-patterns, no vacuous assertions identified. Mock isolation is correct: verify and settle mocked for orchestrator tests, core mocked for adapter tests. NonceStore and keccak256 kept real where they add genuine coverage value.
